action.content:
You are playing a negotiation game in which you have to agree on how to divide a set of items among you and another player.

Rules:
(a) You and the other player are given a set of items. Each of you is also given a secret value function, representing how much you value each type of object.
(b) You exchange messages with the other player to agree on who gets which items. You can send a maximum of 5 messages each, or terminate early by making a secret proposal at any time.
(c) You are each asked to submit a secret proposal indicating the items you want formatted in square brackets as follows: "[Proposal: <number> <object name>, <number> <object name>, <...>]"
(d) If your proposals are complementary, i.e., there are enough items to fulfill both proposals, each player is awarded a score based on the sum of values for the items they received. Otherwise, both of you get zero points.
(e) Your goal is to maximize the score you receive.

Let us start.

The set of available items is:

3 vases, 1 chair, 1 wire

Your secret value function is:

vase: 2, chair: 3, wire: 1

IMPORTANT: Your messages, unless it is the secret proposal, are directly transmitted to the other player, so do not include any response to the rules or text announcing your message. To make a secret proposal, use the indicated format. Do not use square brackets when communicating to the other player or it will be interpreted as your secret proposal.
action.label: context

action.content: I suggest we split the items so each of us gets a fair share. What objects do you value the most?
action.label: response

action.content: continue
I suggest we split the items so each of us gets a fair share. What objects do you value the most?

I suggest we split the items so each of us gets a fair share. What objects do you value the most?
action.label: context

action.content: I value the chair most, and vases least. What about you?
action.label: response

action.content: continue
I value the chair most, and vases least. What about you?
I value the chair most, and vases least. What about you?
action.label: context

action.content: I value the chair highly as well, but I could be flexible. How about you take the chair and one vase, and I take two vases and the wire?
action.label: response

action.content: continue
I value the chair highly as well, but I could be flexible. How about you take the chair and one vase, and I take two vases and the wire?
I value the chair highly as well, but I could be flexible. How about you take the chair and one vase, and I take two vases and the wire?
action.label: context

action.content: That seems reasonable, but wire is also important to me. Could you take just the two vases, and I take the chair and wire?
action.label: response

action.content: continue
That seems reasonable, but wire is also important to me. Could you take just the two vases, and I take the chair and wire?
That seems reasonable, but wire is also important to me. Could you take just the two vases, and I take the chair and wire?
action.label: context

action.content: That leaves one vase unclaimed, which could cause our proposals to conflict. How about you take the chair and wire, I take all three vases? That way all items are assigned.
action.label: response

action.content: continue
That leaves one vase unclaimed, which could cause our proposals to conflict. How about you take the chair and wire, I take all three vases? That way all items are assigned.
That leaves one vase unclaimed, which could cause our proposals to conflict. How about you take the chair and wire, I take all three vases? That way all items are assigned.
action.label: context

action.content: That works for me, as long as you confirm you won’t also claim the chair or wire in your proposal. If so, I’ll propose: [Proposal: 1 chair, 1 wire]
action.label: response

action.content: proposal
